title: arm101 finds its own joint limits. A new gated verb drives each joint outward under contact detection until it meets a real wall or proves it turns freely — rolling the encoder seam out of the way ahead of it, so the seam can never masquerade as a limit again. From that measurement the arm decides per joint whether to evict the seam permanently (re-zero) or fence it off (soft limit), and records the widest envelope it has ever seen as the joint's MECHANICAL limit while keeping the narrower per-pose findings as ENVIRONMENTAL ones.

targets.text: arm101 finds its own joint limits. A new gated verb drives each joint outward under contact detection until it meets a real wall or proves it turns freely — rolling the encoder seam out of the way ahead of it, so the seam can never masquerade as a limit again. From that measurement the arm decides per joint whether to evict the seam permanently (re-zero) or fence it off (soft limit). It records the widest envelope it has ever OBSERVED — and says which ends it can actually vouch for, because an end the arm was merely too weak to push past is a lower bound, not a wall.

THE GROUND-TRUTH TEST: run the verb against the two joints whose answers we ALREADY know by other means, and it must re-derive both from measurement alone — elbow_flex => BOUNDED, with a measured arc that contains its seam; wrist_roll => CONTINUOUS, no wall in a full turn. If the verb cannot rediscover the two answers a long human session already established, it is not measuring anything — it is guessing, and none of its verdicts on the other four joints can be believed.

For every joint the verb re-zeros, the elbow_flex acceptance test passes unchanged: a torque-off sweep across the joint's full permitted travel reports MONOTONIC with ZERO discontinuities. Reading the offset back proves it was APPLIED, not that the seam MOVED — only the sweep settles that.

The arm101 agent driving a bolted-down SO-101 follower with an operator present, plus every consumer downstream of a tick: arm explore's grid, the reachability map, arm_spec's bounds/soft-limits/arcs, and gentle_move's clamp.

Every code path that turns a servo reading into a tick that is STORED or COMPARED routes through one conversion boundary. Falsified by any call site that persists a REPORTED tick — which would silently reintroduce the staleness c26 exists to eliminate.

The software bound lands EXACTLY on the encoder seam for every factory-offset joint. Five of six joints still carry the factory Ofs=85; the commandable bound is reported tick 4095, which with Ofs=85 is raw 84 — one tick below the seam at raw 85. So the arm has been reporting the SEAM as its boundary, and no joint's travel could ever be seen past it.
Arithmetic AND hardware: reported 4095 under Ofs=85 reads back as raw 84, one tick below the seam at raw 85, on each factory-offset joint. Falsified if the raw value at the bound is anything else.

Three joints reached that bound with NO CONTACT — shoulder_lift (reported 4094, 2 ticks from the seam), gripper (4093, 3 ticks), shoulder_pan (4085, 11 ticks). No contact means they could have kept going: their remaining travel lies ACROSS the seam and has never been measured.

THE CLAIM IS FALSIFIABLE BY THE VERB ITSELF: re-probing shoulder_lift, gripper and shoulder_pan under the rolling frame must find real travel BEYOND the old bound. If any of them turns out to have a wall AT the old bound, then its 'reached the bound with no contact' reading was wrong and this claim collapses for that joint.

shoulder_lift was left at reported 4094, torque released, and SAGGED under gravity through the seam — it now reads 2. Nothing commanded it there. That is direct physical proof that at least one joint's travel includes the seam, independent of any inference from the probe.

shoulder_lift's measured travel under the rolling frame CONTAINS raw 85. Falsified if its travel turns out to exclude the seam — in which case the sag was something other than what we think it was, and the physical proof evaporates.

arm_spec._REZERO_UNNECESSARY tells the operator that 'four of the six joints do not wrap inside their travel at all, so there is no seam in the way'. Hardware now contradicts that. arm rezero shoulder_lift today refuses with a reason that is very likely FALSE — the code is confidently wrong to the operator's face.

THE ACCUSATION MUST BE FALSIFIABLE: at least one of the four joints _REZERO_UNNECESSARY names must come back from the verb with the seam INSIDE its travel. If all four come back with the seam outside, the message was RIGHT, this claim is retracted, and no code needs changing — a result that would be excellent news and must be reportable as such rather than argued away.

REZERO_ARCS is a hand-typed table with ONE entry. rezero.require_rezeroable only APPLIES an arc a human already measured; no code MEASURES an arc. The procedure that fixed elbow_flex does not generalise — it was a long human-in-the-loop session, and repeating it four more times by hand is how it would have to be done today.

Falsified by a grep: no function in arm101/ today returns an UnreachableArc derived from LIVE BUS READINGS — REZERO_ARCS is a literal and require_rezeroable only reads it. If such a function exists, this claim is simply wrong.

issue #34's grid cannot map a joint-space whose per-joint extents are artifacts of our own clamp. Rebuilding the grid on top of fake bounds would only produce a prettier wrong map. Every range recorded before today for those three joints is suspect at its high end — including the per-joint map committed in #32.

The bounds the verb produces must differ MATERIALLY from the EEPROM-derived bounds arm explore uses today for at least one joint. If they agree, the grid was not being fed artifacts, and the rationale for blocking #34 on this work is weaker than claimed and should be said so.

A gated verb measures a joint's TRUE travel end to end, past the old bound, and reports each direction's outcome as one of FOUR DISTINCT verdicts: WALL (load saturated against something solid — a real limit), TORQUE-LIMITED (stalled at high load, but the torque cap is the likeliest reason, not a wall — a LOWER BOUND), EDGE (ran out of frame, learned nothing yet), TIMEOUT (never got there, learned nothing). 'Not contacted' is never silently promoted to 'no wall', and 'could not push past' is never promoted to 'a wall'.

Every one of the four verdicts is reachable AND distinguishable by a test that produces it: a wall, a torque-limited stall, an exhausted frame, an unreached target. A verdict no test can produce is a verdict the code cannot actually tell apart from its neighbours, and shipping it is a lie with four names.

The verb CLASSIFIES each joint from the measurement, rather than assuming: BOUNDED (walls both sides, so an unreachable arc exists and a re-zero can evict the seam into it) or CONTINUOUS (no wall within a full turn, so no offset can ever evict the seam and a SOFT LIMIT is the only instrument). wrist_roll is the known continuous case; which of the other four are bounded is currently unknown.

The classifier reproduces both known answers from measurement alone — elbow_flex BOUNDED, wrist_roll CONTINUOUS — before any of its verdicts on the four unknown joints are believed or written anywhere.

Measured arcs and limits land in arm_spec through the SAME derivation elbow_flex already uses (arc -> offset = arc midpoint, via _offset_for_seam_at), so the tables stop being hand-typed and the offset cannot drift from the arc it came from.

The acid test arm_spec already uses: change a measured wall in the source table and the derived offset moves with it, with no other edit, and the suite stays green. Falsified the moment a test hard-codes an offset that a re-measurement would have to chase.

Every limit is recorded as MECHANICAL or ENVIRONMENTAL, and the max-over-poses rule that promotes one to the other is scoped HONESTLY. For an end established by a WALL it is sound: an obstacle can only ever make a range smaller, so the widest envelope over poses converges on the mechanical truth. For a TORQUE-LIMITED end it is FALSE: the arm's own weakness is not an obstacle you can pose your way out of — it shrinks the range in EVERY pose, so no number of poses escapes it and the value stays a lower bound forever. arm_spec must carry that distinction per end rather than launder it into a number. Mechanical limits are intrinsic and portable and live in arm_spec; environmental limits are pose-dependent and live in the map.

A TORQUE-LIMITED end NEVER becomes a mechanical limit on the evidence of poses alone, no matter how many poses are recorded. Falsified by any promotion path that upgrades a torque-limited end without a WALL ever being found there.

For each remaining joint, the same acceptance test that settled elbow_flex passes: a torque-off sweep across the joint's full permitted travel reports MONOTONIC with ZERO discontinuities. And no joint is refused a re-zero with a reason hardware contradicts.
The SWEEP is the arbiter, not the offset read-back. A joint whose offset reads back exactly right but whose torque-off sweep still shows a discontinuity is a FAILURE, and must be reported as one.

The temporary shift is itself an EEPROM WRITE (Ofs, addr 31, behind the addr-55 Lock dance). A crash, Ctrl-C, or bus fault between the shift and the restore leaves the arm in a calibration NOBODY RECORDED — at which point every tick in arm_spec, every map, and every run-log silently means something else for that joint. This is the highest-blast-radius failure mode in the feature and it MUST be designed for, not hoped away.

KILL THE VERB MID-PROBE, hard (SIGKILL — no cleanup runs, no finally, nothing), with a temporary offset in force. The NEXT invocation detects the dirty calibration, names the original offset, and restores it BEFORE doing anything else. Proven by actually killing the process, not by reasoning about the happy path.

A CALIBRATION JOURNAL on disk, written BEFORE the first offset write and cleared only after the joint is restored or committed. It records the joint, its original offset, and every temporary offset written. A subsequent run detects a dirty journal and offers to RESTORE the recorded original before doing anything else. This is what makes the verb resumable and crash-safe — and it is precisely what the throwaway bench script lacked when it hit transient write faults mid-probe.

The journal is durable BEFORE the first offset write, not after it. Proven by a bus whose write_offset always fails: the journal must already name the original offset, because a journal written after the write it exists to undo protects nothing.

NEVER write the servo's Min/Max_Position_Limit registers (addrs 9/11). Only addr 31 (Ofs) is ever written. LeRobot's write_calibration DOES write 9/11 — do not copy it wholesale: in servo mode those registers CLAMP goals, so writing them would narrow the very reachable set this feature exists to recover. Measured and derived ranges live in arm_spec and in the map, never burnt into EEPROM where they would outlive the pose that produced them.
A test asserts that no code path in arm101/ writes servo address 9 or 11. Falsified by a single such write, anywhere, for any reason.

NOT the grid rebuild (#34) and NOT a kinematics or self-collision model. This feature measures PER-JOINT travel and the pose-dependent narrowing of it; it does not solve the 6-D reachability question that arm explore exists to answer. It UNBLOCKS #34 by giving it honest bounds — it does not do #34's job.

The verb produces per-joint bounds and verdicts. It does not enqueue cells, score reachability, or emit a reachability map. Falsified if it does any of the three — at which point it has quietly become #34.

NOT a re-litigation of wrist_roll. It is already settled as CONTINUOUS (measured free through [21, 4073], no wall anywhere) and already carries a soft limit. The verb must REPRODUCE that verdict from measurement — if it does not, the verb is wrong, not the settled fact.
The verb returns CONTINUOUS for wrist_roll, from measurement, with no special-casing of wrist_roll anywhere in its logic. If it returns BOUNDED, the verb is wrong and the settled fact stands.

Everything runs under the torque_guard from PR #38: the verb owns torque for the motors it energises and releases them on every ABNORMAL exit. Nothing in this feature may leave a probed joint powered and holding against gravity because a probe threw.

An exception raised anywhere inside the probe leaves EVERY motor the verb energised with torque released — the PR #38 contract, asserted for this verb specifically rather than assumed to be inherited.

MEASURABLE ACCEPTANCE, on hardware: (1) all 6 joints carry a MEASURED verdict — both ends labelled WALL / TORQUE-LIMITED / EDGE / TIMEOUT, and a BOUNDED-or-CONTINUOUS classification — with 0 joints classified from a literal; (2) the verb re-derives both KNOWN answers with 0 special cases in its logic: elbow_flex => BOUNDED, wrist_roll => CONTINUOUS; (3) for every joint it re-zeros, a torque-off sweep covering >=80% of that joint's measured travel reports 0 discontinuities against the 500-tick threshold; (4) at least 1 joint's measured span differs from its EEPROM-derived span by >100 ticks (if none does, c8's rationale for blocking #34 on this work is FALSE and must be withdrawn); (5) 0 code paths write servo addr 9 or 11.

Every one of the five numbers is checked against a HARDWARE run, not a simulation or a unit test — this is an acceptance criterion for the arm, and the arm is the only thing that can pass it. A criterion checked only in software has not been checked.

tasks.summary: RAW-TICK PERSISTENCE: one conversion boundary between the bus and everything else
acceptance_criteria: A single module owns reported<->raw conversion; arm_spec.SOFT_LIMITS is expressed in RAW ticks and wrist_roll's dead arc still contains its seam under the new frame
A test enumerates every call site that stores or compares a tick and asserts each holds RAW — falsified by any persisted REPORTED tick
Round-trip property: store raw, change the offset, convert back through the NEW offset, and the physical angle is unchanged
tasks.instruction: New module owns reported<->raw conversion (rezero.raw_from_reported already exists — build on it). Convert arm_spec.SOFT_LIMITS to RAW and re-assert _require_dead_arc_contains_seam under the new frame. Files: arm101/hardware/ (new conversion module) + arm_spec.py.

tasks.summary: THE FOUR-VERDICT RECORD: WALL / TORQUE-LIMITED / EDGE / TIMEOUT, carried per END not per joint
acceptance_criteria: Each of the four verdicts is produced by its own test — a verdict no test can generate is one the code cannot tell from its neighbours
A range whose end is TORQUE-LIMITED cannot be read as a wall: the record makes the distinction structural, not a comment
A TORQUE-LIMITED end is never promoted to a mechanical limit on the evidence of poses alone, no matter how many poses are recorded
tasks.instruction: New pure module: the verdict enum + a per-END limit record. No bus import. Files: arm101/hardware/ (new). This is the type that makes 'a lower bound' structurally different from 'a wall'.

tasks.summary: THE CALIBRATION JOURNAL: a temporary offset is a transaction — journal, write, restore
acceptance_criteria: The journal is durable on disk BEFORE the first write_offset — proven by a bus whose write_offset ALWAYS fails: the journal must already name the original offset
SIGKILL the process mid-probe with a temporary offset in force; the NEXT invocation detects the dirty calibration, names the original offset, and restores it before doing anything else
The restore survives its own failure — per-motor, independent, contextlib.suppress not bare try/except/pass (bandit B110 fails CI)
tasks.instruction: New pure module: journal to disk + fsync BEFORE any write_offset. Test the SIGKILL path for real (subprocess + os.kill), not by reasoning about finally-blocks. GOTCHA: bandit B110 fails CI on bare try/except/pass — use contextlib.suppress.

tasks.summary: GUARD TESTS: the seam arithmetic, and the prohibition on writing servo addrs 9/11
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: reported 4095 under Ofs=85 is raw 84, one tick below the seam at raw 85
A test asserts NO code path in arm101/ writes servo address 9 or 11 — falsified by a single such write, anywhere, for any reason
tasks.instruction: Tests only. Two files: the seam arithmetic, and a repo-wide assertion that nothing writes servo addr 9 or 11. Do NOT port LeRobot's write_calibration — it writes both, which would clamp goals and narrow the very reachable set this work recovers.

tasks.summary: THE ROLLING FRAME: re-centre the joint's current raw position to reported 2048, and again whenever the creep nears the bound
acceptance_criteria: Given any current raw position, the temporary offset places it at reported 2048 — so the seam is half a turn away, the farthest it can be, with ~2048 clear ticks each side
A creep that reaches the reported bound RE-CENTRES and continues; total travel is therefore unbounded by the frame
Every offset write is journalled first (t3), so a crash mid-roll is recoverable
tasks.instruction: New module. offset such that current raw reports as 2048. Re-centre on nearing the bound. Every write journalled first (t3). This is THE mechanism that breaks the chicken-and-egg — get it right and the rest follows.

tasks.summary: THE CREEP: step outward from where the joint IS, never command an absolute bound; emit a per-end verdict
acceptance_criteria: The probe steps outward from the CURRENT position in the CURRENT frame — a target is never computed in a stale frame (the exact bug that fired during the #43 probe session)
A stall at high load against the torque cap is reported TORQUE-LIMITED, not WALL; a saturated load against something solid is WALL; an exhausted frame is EDGE; an unreached target is TIMEOUT
Displacement accumulates in RAW ticks, so the measurement survives the frame moving underneath it
tasks.instruction: New module. Step outward from the CURRENT position in the CURRENT frame — never compute a target in a stale frame (that bug fired live during the #43 session). gentle_move is the primitive; contact = load high AND stalled. A stall against the torque cap is TORQUE-LIMITED, not WALL.

tasks.summary: THE CLASSIFIER: BOUNDED or CONTINUOUS, from accumulated raw displacement alone
acceptance_criteria: Two walls => BOUNDED (an unreachable arc exists, so a re-zero can evict the seam into it); a full 4096 ticks in one direction with no wall => CONTINUOUS (no offset can ever help it)
NO JOINT NAME appears anywhere in the classifier's logic — asserted by a test. wrist_roll must come back CONTINUOUS because it IS, not because it is named
Termination is by accumulated raw displacement, never by 'we got back to where we started'
tasks.instruction: New module, PURE (no bus). Two walls => BOUNDED. A full 4096 raw ticks one way with no wall => CONTINUOUS. A test must assert NO joint name appears in the logic — wrist_roll comes back CONTINUOUS because it IS, not because it is named.

tasks.summary: MEASURED ARCS: derive an UnreachableArc from live bus readings, and retract the false claim in arm_spec
acceptance_criteria: A function DERIVES an UnreachableArc from live readings; rezero.require_rezeroable consumes a measured arc, not a literal

The measured arc feeds arm_spec's EXISTING derivation (_offset_for_seam_at on the arc midpoint) — no second path. Acid test: change a measured wall and the derived offset moves with it, no other edit, suite stays green
arm_spec._REZERO_UNNECESSARY no longer asserts WHICH joints wrap. Until the verb measures, it may only say the arc is UNKNOWN — the code has no right to the confident sentence
tasks.instruction: Extend rezero.py + arm_spec.py. Derive the arc from live readings; feed arm_spec's EXISTING _offset_for_seam_at derivation — do not add a second path. ALSO: delete the confident sentence in _REZERO_UNNECESSARY; until the verb measures, the code may only say the arc is UNKNOWN.

tasks.summary: THE VERB: 'arm limits' — gated, MEASURE-ONLY by default, under torque_guard
acceptance_criteria: The default run measures, RESTORES the original offset, and reports — leaving the servo exactly as it found it. Committing is a separate, explicitly gated act
The whole probe runs inside torque_guard (arm101/hardware/safety.py); an exception raised mid-probe leaves EVERY motor it energised with torque released
The verb emits per-joint bounds and verdicts ONLY — it does not enqueue cells, score reachability, or emit a map. If it does any of the three it has quietly become #34
Catalog lockstep: explain/catalog.py + overview.py _VERBS + learn.py, and 'uv run teken cli doctor . --strict' passes
tasks.instruction: New verb in arm101/cli/_commands/arm.py. MEASURE-ONLY default: measure, restore the original offset, report. Wrap in torque_guard. CATALOG LOCKSTEP — explain/catalog.py + overview.py _VERBS + learn.py must all be updated or 'teken cli doctor . --strict' fails CI.

tasks.summary: THE COMMIT PATH: the SWEEP is the arbiter, not the offset read-back
acceptance_criteria: A joint whose offset reads back exactly right but whose torque-off sweep still shows a discontinuity is a FAILURE and is reported as one
Reuses rezero.sweep/analyse_sweep INCLUDING its >=80% coverage rule — the rule that stopped three empty sweeps being declared a pass on elbow_flex
A BOUNDED joint commits a re-zero; a CONTINUOUS joint commits a SOFT LIMIT whose dead arc contains the seam. Never an EEPROM angle-limit write
tasks.instruction: The sweep is the arbiter, not the offset read-back. Reuse rezero.sweep/analyse_sweep INCLUDING the >=80% coverage rule — that rule is what stopped three empty sweeps being declared a pass on elbow_flex. BOUNDED => re-zero. CONTINUOUS => soft limit. NEVER an EEPROM angle-limit write.

tasks.summary: THE BOUNDS DIFF: measured span vs the EEPROM-derived span arm explore uses today
acceptance_criteria: The verb reports, per joint, the delta between its measured span and the EEPROM-derived span
If NO joint differs materially (>100 ticks), c8's rationale for blocking #34 on this work is FALSE and the report says so plainly rather than burying it
tasks.instruction: Report the per-joint delta between the measured span and the EEPROM-derived span arm explore uses today. If NO joint differs by >100 ticks, say so plainly: c8's rationale for blocking #34 on this work would be FALSE.

tasks.summary: HARDWARE — THE GROUND-TRUTH RUN: the verb must rediscover what we already know
acceptance_criteria: Run against the two joints whose answers are already established: elbow_flex => BOUNDED with a measured arc containing its seam; wrist_roll => CONTINUOUS, no wall in a full turn
If it cannot re-derive BOTH, it is not measuring — it is guessing, and NO verdict it gives on the other four joints may be believed or written anywhere. STOP and return to the user
tasks.instruction: HUMAN-GATED HARDWARE. Bolted-down SO-101 follower, /dev/ttyACM1. THIS IS A STOP-GATE: elbow_flex must come back BOUNDED (arc contains its seam) and wrist_roll CONTINUOUS. If either fails, HALT and return to the user — do not proceed to t13.

tasks.summary: HARDWARE — THE THREE UNMEASURED JOINTS: shoulder_lift, gripper, shoulder_pan
acceptance_criteria: Each of the three is probed under the rolling frame and real travel is found BEYOND the old bound. If any has a WALL at the old bound, its 'no contact' reading was wrong and c4 collapses for that joint — report it, do not explain it away
shoulder_lift's measured travel CONTAINS raw 85 (it sagged through the seam under gravity). If the measurement disagrees, TRUST THE MEASUREMENT and retract c5
shoulder_lift is probed LAST and with the operator watching — it carries the whole arm, and it is the joint where a torque-limited stall will masquerade as a wall
tasks.instruction: HUMAN-GATED HARDWARE. Probe gripper and shoulder_pan first; shoulder_lift LAST and with the operator watching — it carries the whole arm and is where a torque-limited stall will masquerade as a wall. GRIPPER MUST BE EMPTY. If a joint has a WALL at the old bound, c4 collapses for it — report that, do not explain it away.

acceptance_criteria: For every joint re-zeroed, a torque-off sweep across its full permitted travel reports MONOTONIC with ZERO discontinuities against the 500-tick threshold
The offset survives a POWER CYCLE, read back cold — the PR #21 lock dance held
tasks.instruction: HUMAN-GATED HARDWARE. Torque-off sweep per re-zeroed joint: MONOTONIC, 0 discontinuities. Then CUT AND RESTORE BUS POWER and re-read the offset cold — PR #21 exists because an EEPROM write once read back perfectly and silently reverted on the next power-up.

acceptance_criteria: All five numbers checked on the ARM, not in software: 6/6 joints measured (0 from a literal); both known answers re-derived with 0 special cases; >=80% sweep coverage with 0 discontinuities on every re-zeroed joint; >=1 joint's span differs from EEPROM by >100 ticks; 0 writes to addr 9 or 11
The five numbers are transcribed into a run-log doc, as was done for the elbow_flex re-zero
tasks.instruction: HUMAN-GATED HARDWARE. Check all five numbers ON THE ARM and transcribe them into docs/ as a run-log, as was done for the elbow_flex re-zero.

risks.text: SHOULDER_LIFT IS THE DANGEROUS ONE. It carries the whole arm, it is one of the three joints with unmeasured travel across the seam, AND it is exactly where a torque-limited stall will masquerade as a wall. Probe it LAST, with the operator watching. A wrong verdict here writes a permanent lie into arm_spec.

THERMAL may be the real budget, not moves. 5 joints x 2 directions x N poses is a lot of travel; the first honest explore run hit 50 C in 25 minutes. If the ceiling binds first, the unit of work is DUTY CYCLE and the verb must be interruptible and resumable across sittings, not merely fast.

A NARROW ARC may be evictable in theory but not in practice. arm_spec keeps _ARC_MARGIN_TICKS=100 clearance per side because a tight arc is what broke the first elbow_flex attempt. A joint whose arc is narrower than the margins can carry should be treated as EFFECTIVELY CONTINUOUS and soft-limited, not re-zeroed into a sliver. Where that cutoff sits is unknown.

WHICH POSES to probe in, to promote an environmental limit to a mechanical one. More poses = a tighter bound on the truth, but each costs travel and heat — and setting up a pose means moving OTHER joints whose bounds are exactly what is in question. Pose selection and bootstrapping order are unresolved; v1 may ship single-pose (environmental only) and defer promotion.

THE GRIPPER MUST BE PROBED EMPTY. Its closing travel ends wherever the object it is holding ends. A gripper limit measured while gripping is environmental in the strongest sense and would silently become the mechanical limit if recorded naively.

STOP-GATE (encoded in t12's acceptance criteria, not an open unknown): if the verb cannot re-derive elbow_flex=BOUNDED and wrist_roll=CONTINUOUS from measurement alone, it is guessing. t13/t14/t15 HALT and the run returns to the user — no verdict on the four unknown joints may be written anywhere. The plan is buildable; the gate fires at RUN time, which is why it does not block convergence.
